justifications.completeness: Only one of four pedestrians and one of five vehicles were reported, missing the majority of annotated objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the reported entities many attributes match ground truth (actions, context, motion and door/trunk states), but many other entities and finer details are missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided despite multiple nearby objects and pedestrian-road relations present in ground truth contexts.
justifications.overall: The extraction correctly labels attributes for a single person and vehicle but fails to detect most actors and provides no spatial relations, limiting its usefulness for scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses all annotated pedestrians and most vehicles from ground truth, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some vehicle attributes like type and motion status but omits many ground truth labels such as pedestrian actions and several vehicle objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a couple of spatial relations which are plausible but sparse and do not cover pedestrians or most vehicles.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction contains some useful semantic details for a subset of objects but fails to detect multiple ground truth entities and many attributes, resulting in a low overall score.

justifications.completeness: The extraction identifies one pedestrian and one vehicle but misses three additional pedestrians and multiple vehicles present in the ground truth, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the detected objects the model provides several relevant attributes such as atomic action, context, type and motion status, but it does not enumerate or distinguish the majority of objects in the scene.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relation fields are left empty despite textual hints about left placement, so explicit structured spatial relations are essentially missing.
justifications.overall: The extraction gives some useful semantic attributes for a few detected entities but fails to comprehensively detect and relate the many pedestrians and vehicles in the scene, resulting in poor overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian and two vehicles were reported versus seven pedestrians and six vehicles in the ground truth, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute fields (actions, context, age, door/trunk states) but omits most individuals and contains some incorrect or mismatched motion labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial_relations fields are empty despite track hints indicating left/center placement and several pedestrians described as on or along the road, so explicit spatial relations are not captured.
justifications.overall: Missing the majority of entities and lacking explicit spatial relations results in low overall quality despite moderate per-entity attribute detail for the few extracted objects.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 7 pedestrians and 1 of 4 vehicles, omitting the majority of objects and track identifiers from the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes (action, context, vehicle state) but misses important class-specific labels and contains incorrect context and motion labels compared to ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided despite multiple nearby actors and scene layout information present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is poor due to very low recall of objects, missing track associations, and several incorrect or incomplete semantic annotations.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 5 ground-truth pedestrians and 1 of 5 vehicles, indicating major omissions.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction includes useful attribute types (actions, context, age, motion_status, door/trunk) but these are provided for very few objects and several ground-truth labels (e.g., a talking pedestrian, multiple vehicle motion states) are missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial_relations entries were produced despite positional hints, so spatial relationships are largely absent.
justifications.overall: Some attributes are correct and relevant, but the system misses most objects and spatial information, yielding low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detected only 2 of 7 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles, missing multiple people and mischaracterizing vehicle states, so coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the objects it did detect the VLM provided a broad set of semantic attributes (actions, context, communicative, transporting, age), giving moderate richness despite missing entities and some context mismatches.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are left empty for all entities and positional/context labels are applied inconsistently (side of road vs on road vs jaywalking not captured), resulting in very limited spatial information.
justifications.overall: Major omissions in object detection and lack of spatial details outweigh the reasonable per-object attribute coverage, yielding low overall utility for driving-scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 6 pedestrians and 2 of 5 vehicles, missing multiple objects and many correct motion labels.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides useful attributes like actions, simple context, and vehicle properties but omits several contextual labels (e.g., talking in group, jaywalking) and object identifiers.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported and several simple context labels conflict with ground truth (many pedestrians labeled 'on the road' in GT vs 'along the side' in extraction), so spatial detail is essentially absent.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains some accurate attribute-level information but misses most objects and key contextual/spatial details, resulting in limited utility for perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Only 2 of 4 pedestrians and 2 of 5 vehicles from the ground truth are reported, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides useful attributes (actions, age, door/trunk status) but omits several contextual and communicative labels and many vehicle instances, limiting overall semantic coverage.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial_relations arrays are empty despite track hints and some relative descriptions, so formal spatial information is largely absent.
justifications.overall: The output captures a subset of entities with some attributes but misses numerous objects and important labels, resulting in a poor overall extraction.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 7 people and 1 of 4 vehicles, missing the majority of actors in the frame.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes are correct (atomic actions, parked) but several labels are incorrect or invented (e.g., transporting as carrying, waiting to cross) and many entities lack annotations.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided despite multiple nearby pedestrians and vehicles, leaving positional context unaddressed.
justifications.overall: Sparse extraction with a few partially accurate labels but major omissions and several incorrect attributes result in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 5 pedestrians and 1 of 3 vehicles, missing the majority of entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided detailed attributes for the single detected pedestrian but missed contextual labels like 'jaywalking' for other pedestrians and misreported vehicle motion (stopped vs moving/parked).
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided and important positional/contextual distinctions (e.g., jaywalking vs walking along the side) were not captured.
justifications.overall: Captures a few correct attributes for one pedestrian and one vehicle but omits most entities and several key contextual labels, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 5 pedestrians and 1 of 3 vehicles, missing several actors.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided basic attributes (actions, simple context, age, motion) for detected agents but omitted many contextual distinctions and did not link to ground-truth ids.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported despite ground-truth distinctions such as jaywalking versus walking along the road.
justifications.overall: Captures a few correct attributes for some agents but misses the majority of objects, lacks spatial relations, and partially misrepresents contextual details.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of four pedestrians and one of three vehicles, missing most entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple semantic attributes (age, actions, context) for detected objects but has limited coverage and mislabels a van as a car.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported, so relative positions and interactions are entirely missing.
justifications.overall: Limited detection coverage and missing spatial information outweigh correct attribute labeling, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 3 of 8 ground truth entities (2 of 5 pedestrians and 1 of 3 vehicles), so many relevant objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the detected objects the extraction captures several semantic attributes correctly (actions, age, motion, trunk and door states) though it misses finer context distinctions for some pedestrians and omits attributes for missing objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided while some ground truth context implies spatial positioning, so this dimension is entirely missing.
justifications.overall: The extraction shows reasonable attribute labeling for the few detected entities but poor object coverage and no spatial relations, resulting in a low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 4 pedestrians and 1 of 2 vehicles (missing multiple persons and one vehicle), giving low coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the objects detected the extraction provides many attributes (actions, context, age, motion, door/trunk states), but it omits several other pedestrians and vehicle instances so overall richness is moderate.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are left empty despite track hints and contextual cues in the ground truth, so spatial relation information is lacking.
justifications.overall: Moderate attribute detail for detected objects but poor recall of objects and missing spatial relations lead to a below-average overall score.

justifications.completeness: Detected one of eight pedestrians and the vehicle but missed most person instances and did not provide track identifiers.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes for the detected pedestrian and vehicle such as action, context, age, and motion status but omits finer-grained labels present in the ground truth and multiple entity coverage.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported despite ground truth indicating side-of-road and jaywalking contexts.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a few correct attributes for a very small subset of objects but is largely incomplete and lacks spatial relation detail, limiting its usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Misses one pedestrian (the bicyclist) and at least two vehicles including the stopped vehicle, and omits jaywalking context so coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some object attributes like age, color hints, and trunk and door states but misses important contextual labels like biking and jaywalking and does not identify all vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Leaves spatial_relations arrays empty and fails to encode relations like pedestrian on road or vehicles on left despite track hints, so spatial annotation is minimal.
justifications.overall: Partial and somewhat informative extraction but with significant omissions and missing spatial and contextual labels leading to limited utility.

justifications.completeness: The extraction correctly identifies one pedestrian and one parked vehicle but misses an additional person (sitting/biking) and a moving vehicle, yielding roughly half of the objects present.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extracted entities include several attribute labels (atomic action, age, trunk/doors) and a vehicle type, but important contextual labels and one object's actions are missing or not captured.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations arrays are empty despite a track hint mentioning 'on the right', so explicit spatial relation annotations are largely absent.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partially accurate with correct labels for some objects but omits key objects and explicit spatial relations, resulting in moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The model detected both people and matched ages and one walking action but missed vehicle detections and the cyclist's atomic action, so coverage is partial.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes like simple context and age are provided but key labels such as the cyclist's atomic action and any vehicle properties are missing, limiting richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported despite contextual cues like walking on the road and relative positions to vehicles, resulting in poor spatial detail.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures core pedestrian presence and some context but omits vehicles and several important attributes, yielding an overall low to moderate quality.

justifications.completeness: The model correctly detected two people matching the count in ground truth but missed all vehicles and therefore omitted important object classes.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides a few basic attributes (action, context, age) but contains incorrect or inconsistent action labels and omits many vehicle attributes present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: No structured spatial relations were provided, but situational hints about left/right are present and the ground truth contains no explicit spatial relations to require, so the impact is minor.
justifications.overall: While some pedestrian information is captured, key errors in actions and the complete omission of vehicles result in a low overall quality for driving-scene perception.

justifications.completeness: Misses the detected cyclist and most vehicles, reporting only a single van and no pedestrians.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attribute details for one van but lacks person attributes, omits most vehicle instances, and misreports motion status.
justifications.spatial_relations: Does not populate spatial_relations even though the scene_context describes an approaching cyclist and van.
justifications.overall: Sparse and partially incorrect extraction that misses key actors and many vehicles while providing few reliable semantics.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one vehicle and missed the cyclist and multiple other vehicles, so the extraction is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a few vehicle attributes (type, trunk/doors) but omits the person and most vehicle instances and attributes, yielding low semantic richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided despite contextual cues, so spatial information is missing.
justifications.overall: Major omissions of the cyclist and most vehicles, incorrect or incomplete motion and attribute reporting, and lack of spatial relations lead to a low overall score.

justifications.completeness: The extraction found 2 of about 8 pedestrians and no vehicles, missing the majority of actors and many annotated attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: It reports basic atomic actions and simple contexts for two people but omits key attributes such as transporting and communicative cues present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are left empty despite multiple ground truth distinctions like walking on the road versus along the side of the road and an overall sidewalk location.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is poor due to many missed actors, missing vehicles, and several incorrect or omitted attributes despite a partly correct scene description.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 pedestrians and no vehicles compared to 6 persons and 2 vehicles in the ground truth, missing several actors and associated attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic atomic actions and age labels but omits many key attributes from the ground truth such as transporting details, communicative cues, and vehicle properties.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations arrays are empty and the scene context incorrectly asserts a crosswalk while the ground truth indicates several subjects are jaywalking or simply on the road.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a few basic actors and actions but omits many entities and rich contextual attributes, resulting in low overall fidelity to the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 7 pedestrians and no vehicles, missing multiple tracked persons and all vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes like actions and age but misses many transport states and communicative labels and omits vehicle semantics, limiting richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations provided despite several pedestrians annotated as walking along the side of the road.
justifications.overall: Extraction is sparse and incomplete, capturing a small subset of pedestrians with some attributes but failing to report most actors, vehicle information, and spatial relations.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 3 of 8 pedestrians and no vehicles, missing the majority of actors in the scene.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes multiple per-person attributes (action, context, communicative, transporting, age) but several attributes are incorrect or incomplete and vehicle semantics are omitted.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial_relations are provided and only informal track hints like 'left/center/right' appear, so precise spatial relation information is effectively missing.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a few pedestrians with some attributes but contains misassignments and omits vehicles, resulting in poor overall coverage and accuracy.

justifications.completeness: Extraction missed half of the annotated pedestrians and all vehicles, so coverage of annotated entities is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some useful labels (crosswalk, directing, atomic actions) are present but important attributes like jaywalking and transporting (pushing) are omitted or mismatched.
justifications.spatial_relations: Although the scene location is given, spatial_relations is empty and the output fails to specify relations between pedestrians, vehicles, and crosswalk boundaries.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains some correct semantic elements but suffers from limited entity recall, missing vehicles, and lack of explicit spatial relationships, resulting in low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected one pedestrian and missed five other people and all vehicles, so the extraction is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attribute fields for the detected pedestrian but misses transporting detail, mislabels the scene with a traffic officer, and omits attributes for most actors.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations provided despite a crowded scene with multiple people and vehicles, so spatial context is not captured.
justifications.overall: Captures one adult walking on the road correctly but omits most actors and vehicles and includes incorrect scene information, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Identifies two pedestrians but misses two other people present and fails to report any of the three vehicles, so coverage is very incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a few labels (walking, pushing) but omits or mislabels atomic actions and complex context for multiple persons and contains no vehicle attributes, so semantic detail is limited.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported and relative positions between actors and vehicles were not captured.
justifications.overall: Extraction is sparse and incomplete, missing multiple people, all vehicles, and spatial relations while providing only a few correct labels.

justifications.completeness: The extraction identifies only 2 pedestrians out of 7 annotated people and omits all vehicles, showing very low coverage of objects in the scene.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM captured basic atomic actions and simple context for two subjects but missed many important attributes such as multiple transporting behaviors, biking, jaywalking, and any vehicle states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are left empty despite ground truth containing positional cues like along the side of the road and jaywalking, so spatial information is largely missing.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction provides a small amount of correct information for two pedestrians but fails to capture most persons, their transport interactions, and all vehicles, resulting in poor overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 6 pedestrians and no vehicles, missing several pedestrians and the parked vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several semantic fields like actions and context but omits track ids, mislabels transporting for one person, and misses one pedestrian's pushing attribute, reducing richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Offers no spatial relation annotations or track identifiers while the frame requires relative placement and object IDs.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures some basic attributes for two pedestrians but misses most entities, specific transport actions, and vehicle information, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one pedestrian and no vehicles while the ground truth contains five persons and three vehicles, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes are provided (action, age, simple context) but key labels like multiple atomic actions, transporting states, and vehicle information are missing and a traffic officer is hallucinated.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial relations are given and only one simple context matches a ground truth entry, so spatial detail is largely absent.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is poor due to many missing actors and vehicle annotations, some incorrect elements, and minimal spatial or contextual detail.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 pedestrians and no vehicles versus 10 persons and 5 vehicles in the ground truth, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes (atomic action, age) for the two detected people but misses many actors and several contextual/transporting details and some labels appear incorrect or oversimplified, so semantic detail is limited.
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial_relations fields are empty and the extraction fails to capture distinctions like 'walking along the side of the road' versus 'walking on the road', providing poor spatial information.
justifications.overall: Captures a couple of superficial person attributes but misses the majority of entities, vehicles, and important contextual and spatial details, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 7 pedestrians and 1 of 4 vehicles, missing the majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Captured some attributes like atomic_action, simple_context, and age for detected entities but several actions are incorrect or missing and many entities lack any labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial_relations were provided despite contextual spatial cues in the scene, so spatial relation extraction is absent.
justifications.overall: The extraction is sparse and partially correct for a few entities but misses most objects and spatial relations, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one pedestrian and two vehicles compared to six persons and six vehicles in the ground truth, missing the majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes such as age and atomic action for one pedestrian and motion status for a van are correct, but many objects are omitted, a bicycle is miscategorized, and numerous vehicle attributes are absent.
justifications.spatial_relations: All spatial_relations arrays are empty despite contextual location information in the scene and multiple objects that would merit relational descriptions.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a few correct attributes for a small subset of objects but omits most entities and relational details, yielding low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Only 2 out of 6 ground-truth persons and 1 vehicle were reported, so recall of objects in the scene is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the few detected actors the extraction provides some attributes (actions, context, age) but misses key entities (the cyclist) and per-object identifiers and many vehicle entries, reducing richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction leaves 'spatial_relations' empty for all entries and provides no track linkage to ground-truth, so spatial/contextual relationships are largely absent.
justifications.overall: Low object recall combined with partial attribute annotation and absent spatial relations yields poor overall extraction quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 7 pedestrians and 1 of 3 vehicles, missing several actors and key attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes like actions and ages but omits transporting/complex context for most people and mislabels a senior as adult.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial_relations entries and positional relationships between entities are not provided.
justifications.overall: Captures a few basic characteristics but is largely incomplete and lacks spatial detail, yielding low overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: The extraction finds only 2 of 5 ground-truth pedestrians and the vehicle, missing three persons and one carrying instance and mislabeling a senior as an adult.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output captures basic attributes like atomic action and simple context but omits several actors, a transporting annotation, and an age distinction, limiting semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations are provided but the ground truth contains none either, so the omission is acceptable though no additional relational detail is offered.
justifications.overall: Partial correct detections and some useful attributes are outweighed by missing pedestrians and attribute errors, resulting in below average overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected one of three pedestrians and the single vehicle, missing two people present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Captures correct actions and vehicle state for the detected entities but misses the other pedestrians and mischaracterizes location/context (e.g., 'sidewalk' vs ground truth contexts).
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides no explicit spatial_relation entries linking the pedestrian and truck despite the scene description indicating the pedestrian is passing a parked truck.
justifications.overall: Partially accurate for the detected vehicle and one pedestrian but incomplete coverage of pedestrians and lacking spatial relation annotations lowers overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Only 3 of 8 ground-truth pedestrians are reported and several persons and their attributes are omitted, though the parked vehicle is detected.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute fields but contains incorrect age labels, misses a communicative action (talking on phone), and misassigns transporting for at least one person.
justifications.spatial_relations: All spatial_relations arrays are empty so no relative positioning between agents is provided despite scene context.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is poor because of large omissions in pedestrian detection and several attribute errors, even though the vehicle state is correctly reported.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 6 ground-truth persons (about 33%) and missed several pedestrians including those carrying items or standing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic labels like action and age but misses important attributes (e.g., transporting) and omits communicative cues present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Ground truth contains no spatial relation annotations and the extraction likewise reports none, so this aspect is correct.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures only a small subset of actors with limited attribute accuracy despite correctly omitting spatial relations, yielding a low to moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of six ground truth persons and omitted several actors and their attributes, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes basic labels (walking, age, simple context) for a single pedestrian but misses many per-person attributes present in the ground truth such as carrying and talking on phone, limiting semantic depth.
justifications.spatial_relations: No inter-object spatial relations were reported and the scene location sidewalk partially conflicts with ground truth contexts, so spatial relation information is poor.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures one pedestrian with some basic attributes but fails to detect most actors, important attributes, and relations, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction only captures one out of five pedestrians and misses several actors and their attributes, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The single detected pedestrian has multiple attributes annotated, but the model omits important semantics for several other people such as transporting and phone use found in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations are provided despite multiple nearby pedestrians and differing contexts, so this information is entirely missing.
justifications.overall: Low coverage of actors combined with missing relations and several key attributes results in poor overall extraction quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 6 ground-truth pedestrians and no vehicles (2 of 8 total objects), missing multiple people and both vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided many attribute fields and correctly matched some labels for the two detected pedestrians (atomic action, transporting, age) but omitted attributes for most ground-truth persons and mischaracterized one person's simple context.
justifications.spatial_relations: Returned empty spatial_relations for all detections, so relative positioning information is not captured despite a multi-person scene where such relations would be useful.
justifications.overall: Low completeness and missing spatial relations limit overall utility even though per-object attribute labeling for the two detected pedestrians is reasonably detailed; score is an average of the criteria.

justifications.completeness: Captured 2 of 12 annotated entities (5 persons and 7 vehicles), missing most pedestrians and all vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes like atomic_action and age for identified persons but misses communicative and transporting labels for most pedestrians and any vehicle attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: Produced no explicit spatial_relations entries and only implicit context strings, failing to encode relations present or useful for situational awareness.
justifications.overall: Low overall coverage and limited attribute detail result in poor performance despite some correct atomic actions.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 4 people and no vehicles, missing several actions and transporting annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes like communicative and age but omits transporting for detected persons, complex contextual labels, and any vehicle properties.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported and simple context entries are incomplete or inconsistent with the ground truth road-side annotations.
justifications.overall: Captures a few correct attributes but misses multiple entities, key labels, and vehicle information, yielding low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of two ground-truth persons and omitted the second person and its actions, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided a few basic attributes (standing, adult) for one person but missed communicative and transporting labels and entirely omitted the other person's actions, so semantic detail is poor.
justifications.spatial_relations: Ground truth contains no spatial relations and the extraction likewise has none, so this aspect matches.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures one person's basic pose and age but misses an entire person and several important communicative and transporting labels, yielding a low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected and correctly described one of two pedestrians but missed the second pedestrian and the parked vehicle, so only partially complete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided several correct attributes for the detected person (atomic action, transporting, age) and a scene description but omitted attributes for the missing entities and vehicle details.
justifications.spatial_relations: Did not populate spatial_relations despite a track hint mentioning position and failed to report relations to the parked vehicle, yielding poor spatial information.
justifications.overall: Accurate for the single detected person but missing an entire pedestrian and the vehicle, resulting in moderate overall performance.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 2 vehicles out of 7 and omits the single pedestrian, so it captures a small fraction of annotated objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the two detected vehicles the model provides type and door/trunk/motion states, but it omits person attributes and many vehicle instances and lacks richer contextual labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single vague relation 'next to the building' is given for both vehicles, with no precise positioning or relations between objects or to the pedestrian.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains some useful vehicle-level semantics for two objects but misses most objects and the pedestrian, and provides limited spatial/contextual information.

justifications.completeness: Misses both pedestrians from the ground truth and reports only two of three ground-truth vehicles, so substantial entities are omitted.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic vehicle attributes (motion, trunk, doors) but omits all human actions, ages, and contextual labels present in the ground truth, reducing semantic depth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes generic 'next to the building' relations for vehicles which is plausible, but lacks any spatial relations for the pedestrians and lacks precision.
justifications.overall: Partial and somewhat accurate vehicle information is offset by missing pedestrian detections and limited semantic/contextual detail, resulting in only partially useful extraction.

justifications.completeness: Detected one of two pedestrians and one of two vehicles, missing one person and one vehicle so coverage is limited.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple attributes (actions, context, age, trunk/door states) showing moderate semantic detail but lacks object IDs and some contextual labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives plausible relations (person near truck, truck next to building) that align with the scene description, though ground truth lacks explicit spatial tags to fully verify.
justifications.overall: Reasonably accurate for the objects it reports but incomplete in detection coverage, yielding moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: All annotated entities are present and most attributes are provided, but the second person's simple_context was missed.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute fields and scene description but invents closed/open vehicle attributes and omits the second person's context, reducing reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations fields are empty despite context mentioning left side and potential relations between pedestrians and the bicycle, so relational info is missing.
justifications.overall: Good coverage and mostly correct labels for actions and motion, but missing a key context for the bending person and adding unsupported vehicle attributes lowers overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Both pedestrians are detected with correct core actions and ages, but the extractor missed the 'closing' simple context for the bending person and added a spurious vehicle not present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides useful attributes (actions, ages, contexts for one walker) and overly detailed vehicle properties, but it fails to capture the specific 'closing' context and includes an unwarranted vehicle entry.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial_relations are provided and the provided simple_contexts only partially describe location (road vs 'along the side of the road' missing).
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: correct detection of the two people and most actions but missing a key contextual label and producing a false positive vehicle, reducing overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: The extraction includes the pedestrian and vehicle and most pedestrian attributes, but omits object IDs and gives a vague vehicle type while slightly mismatching motion status.
justifications.semantic_richness: Pedestrian semantics are detailed (action, context, age) but vehicle semantics are weaker (type other, closed vs unknown fields) reducing richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Neither the extraction nor the ground truth contain spatial relations, so providing an empty spatial_relations field is correct.
justifications.overall: Accurate and detailed for the pedestrian and acceptable for the vehicle, but overall quality is reduced by missing IDs and minor vehicle attribute mismatches.

justifications.completeness: Only 2 pedestrians were reported versus ~47 annotated and the single vehicle motion status contradicts ground truth, so the extraction is highly incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes (age, communicative, transporting) are present, but many actors and key labels (multiple communicative states, complex contexts) are missing or unreported.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial relations are provided despite a crosswalk scene and many proximate pedestrians that warrant relational annotations.
justifications.overall: The extraction is sparse and contains factual errors (pedestrian count and vehicle motion), limiting its reliability for driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of many pedestrians and 1 of 2 vehicles, missing the majority of actors in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes like atomic action and age for the few detected actors but omits many contextual labels present in the ground truth such as group communication and varied age categories.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were reported, so relational information is absent despite its importance for scene understanding.
justifications.overall: Extraction contains some correct attribute labels for a few objects but is very sparse, missing most actors and relational context from the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only two pedestrians and one vehicle, missing the majority of annotated persons and one vehicle, so coverage is very poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides useful attributes like atomic actions, simple context, and age for reported entities but omits most entities and some communicative labels present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided for any entities despite opportunities to indicate crossing and relative positions, so spatial detail is lacking.
justifications.overall: The extraction contains correct attribute annotations for a few detections but misses most pedestrians, one vehicle, and spatial relations, yielding low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Extraction detected only three pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth contains over twenty persons and a moving vehicle, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes basic attributes like atomic action, simple context, and age for a few people but omits many communicative labels, age variations, and all vehicle attributes present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial_relations field is empty despite clear relative positions and groupings in the scene, so no spatial information is provided.
justifications.overall: Severe under-detection of entities combined with missing vehicle and spatial details results in poor overall extraction quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only two pedestrians and omits the vast majority of annotated people as well as the single vehicle, so coverage is very poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides multiple attribute fields (action, context, transporting, age) but lacks object IDs, contains incorrect or inconsistent context labels, and misses many annotations, reducing its semantic reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial_relations were provided despite the scene containing clear crossing vs sidewalk placement and inter-object relationships in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: The output captures a couple of pedestrians with attribute detail but misses most actors including the vehicle and contains multiple semantic and contextual errors, leading to a low overall assessment.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only three pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth contains many more people and a parked vehicle, so coverage is very poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides several attribute fields (atomic action, communicative, age, transporting) but many entities and attributes are missing or mismatched against the detailed ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are left empty for all detected pedestrians and the scene-level location is oversimplified, failing to reflect individual context differences present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Due to very low entity recall, partial and sometimes incorrect attribute labeling, and absent spatial relations, the extraction is substantially below acceptable quality for driving-scene perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two pedestrians and no vehicles while ground truth contains over thirty persons and multiple vehicles, so coverage is very poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided basic attributes (atomic action, simple context, age) for the two detected people but missed many entities and finer labels present in ground truth such as jaywalking flags, specific transporting types, communicative details, and any vehicle attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: Left spatial_relations empty and failed to mark important spatial/contextual cues like jaywalking or walking on the road, indicating minimal spatial understanding.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction contains a few correct basic labels for two pedestrians but is sparse, missing the vast majority of entities, spatial relations, and vehicle information from the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Extraction reports only three pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth lists around 30+ persons and one vehicle, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes several attribute fields (atomic_action, simple_context, transporting, age) but misses many nuanced labels (e.g., numerous jaywalking annotations, communicative states) and contains some incorrect contexts.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial_relations are provided in the extraction despite the scene containing road-side vs on-road and jaywalking behaviors that imply spatial relations.
justifications.overall: The output captures a few basic attributes for a small subset of pedestrians but omits most entities and many important semantic distinctions, yielding poor overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two pedestrians and no vehicles while ground truth lists dozens of persons and a vehicle, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic labels like walking and adult but misses crucial contextual labels such as many jaywalking instances, communicative actions, and transporting attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided despite important context about pedestrians crossing versus walking along the road.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures minimal basic attributes but misses most entities and critical contextual and spatial information, yielding poor overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two pedestrians and no vehicles while ground truth lists many persons and four vehicles, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracted a few attributes like action, communicative state, and age but missed many contextual labels such as jaywalking and carrying for multiple people, yielding limited semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations were provided and scene context labels are incomplete or misleading, so spatial understanding is lacking.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures minimal semantics for two individuals but omits most actors, vehicles, and important contextual relations, resulting in poor overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captures only two pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth lists many more persons and a vehicle, so most relevant objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides basic attributes (action, simple context, age) for the two detected pedestrians but omits many ground-truth labels such as jaywalking, sitting, various communicative and transporting states for the other actors.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial_relations fields are empty despite ground truth distinguishing pedestrians on the road versus along the side, so important relative position information is missing.
justifications.overall: The extraction correctly labels a couple of pedestrians with basic attributes but is sparse, misses most actors and the vehicle, and omits key contextual and spatial details, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction reports only two pedestrians and one vehicle versus 23 persons and two vehicles in the ground truth, missing the vast majority of actors and many transport attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction captures basic atomic actions and simple contexts for a couple of people and vehicle state but omits many specific labels present in the ground truth such as multiple walking actors, carrying, talking in group, and phone-looking behaviors.
justifications.spatial_relations: All spatial_relations fields are left empty and the extraction fails to record distinctions like walking on the road versus along the side, losing important spatial placement details from the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Severe under-detection of pedestrians combined with missing relational and behavioral details results in a largely incomplete and low-quality extraction for this scene.

justifications.completeness: The extraction only lists two pedestrians out of many present and misclassifies a motorcyclist as a vehicle while partially matching vehicle states, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: While the extraction includes attribute fields (age, transporting, motion_status), it misses many contextual labels from the ground truth (crossing, talking, looking into phone) and contains at least one incorrect transporting label.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial_relations are empty despite the ground truth containing important location/context distinctions (pedestrian crossing, along the side of the road), so relational information is nearly absent.
justifications.overall: Given the severe under-detection of pedestrians, some misclassifications, and sparse contextual and spatial detail, the extraction quality is poor overall.

justifications.completeness: Very low coverage: only 3 pedestrians and 2 vehicles were reported versus 15 persons and 4 vehicles in the ground truth, missing the majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes useful attribute fields (actions, context, transporting, age, door/trunk states) but contains clear misclassifications (e.g., motorcyclist treated as a pedestrian) and omits many contextual labels from the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Essentially absent: spatial_relations arrays are empty and the extraction fails to capture crossing, jaywalking, or other relative placement information present in the annotations.
justifications.overall: Overall quality is low due to many missed detections and several incorrect or missing semantic/contextual labels despite a reasonable attribute schema.

justifications.completeness: Very low coverage: the VLM reported only 2 pedestrians while the ground truth lists many more and missed one of three vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Moderate attribute coverage since the VLM provided multiple fields (actions, context, age, transport, vehicle states) but many entities are missing and some attribute values are incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Poor spatial detail: the spatial_relations arrays are empty and the extraction does not capture crossing/relative groupings indicated in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is low due to severe under-detection of pedestrians and some incorrect or missing semantic and spatial information despite having useful attribute types.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two pedestrians out of many present while both vehicles were reported, so overall coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides multiple semantic attributes per detected entity (actions, context, age, trunk/door state) but misses many actors and some context granularity.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial_relations were reported for any objects despite clear relative positions in the scene.
justifications.overall: Reasonable per-entity semantics are outweighed by very low pedestrian coverage and absent spatial relations, yielding poor overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only three pedestrians and two vehicles while the ground truth contains dozens of persons and three vehicle annotations, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some useful attributes are present (atomic actions, simple context, age, vehicle state) but many entities lack labels, several contextual details are incorrect or omitted, and multiple ground-truth categories are not represented.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial_relation entries were provided despite many relative positions and interactions apparent in the scene.
justifications.overall: The extraction shows limited and partially accurate semantic detail for a tiny subset of objects but fails on coverage and spatial information, yielding poor overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 pedestrians and 2 vehicles while the ground truth contains many more pedestrians (dozens) and 3 vehicles, so the extraction misses most entities.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes useful attributes (atomic action, simple context, age, motion status) for the few detected objects but omits many complex/contextual labels and attributes present in the ground truth and applies to very few instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: The spatial_relations fields are left empty (only informal track_hints give vague left/right cues), so explicit spatial relationships and placements are essentially missing.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is low due to large omissions of pedestrians and one vehicle plus missing spatial relations, despite moderately detailed semantics for the few detected objects.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses the second person (the bicyclist), mislabels that person as a vehicle, and gets the sitting person's age wrong, so key entities and attributes are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: While multiple attributes are provided, there are critical errors such as misclassifying the cyclist as a vehicle, incorrect age labeling, and omission of the biking simple context for the rider.
justifications.spatial_relations: Structured spatial_relations are empty matching the ground truth annotations, though informal positional hints appear in track_hint.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures some scene elements and motion status but contains significant misclassifications and omissions that reduce its overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Missing the annotated moving vehicle and several person labels are incorrect or swapped (actions and ages), so many ground-truth elements are absent or wrong.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction lists multiple attributes per person but misclassifies key semantic labels (ages and atomic actions) and omits vehicle attributes, reducing overall semantic fidelity.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations are reported but the ground truth includes none either, so the absence is acceptable though relational detail like person-on-bicycle was not provided.
justifications.overall: Critical omissions and mislabeling (missing vehicle, incorrect person actions and ages) lead to a low overall evaluation despite some correct attribute reporting.

justifications.completeness: Both the person and vehicle are reported but several key ground-truth attributes such as biking/sitting and senior age are missing or mislabeled.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction adds descriptive details (e.g., shirt color, bicycle) but many semantic labels are incorrect, reducing its useful semantic accuracy.
justifications.spatial_relations: No explicit spatial_relations entries were provided and the track hints are inconsistent, so spatial relation information is effectively missing.
justifications.overall: Entities are detected but critical attribute errors (action and age) and absent spatial relations result in poor to moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Most attributes are present and correct (action, contexts, no vehicles), but age is incorrect and there is no object track id.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides descriptive scene context and multiple attributes but uses a generic age label and omits a precise track identifier, reducing richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are left empty in the pedestrian entry despite the scene description noting the person is against a wall, so relational detail is missing.
justifications.overall: Accurate on core attributes like action and context but weakened by incorrect age and missing spatial and tracking details.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures the person and most attributes (action, context flags) but omits a track id and mislabels age as adult instead of senior over 65.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes several semantic labels and a scene description but contains an incorrect age attribute and lacks the ground-truth track identifier.
justifications.spatial_relations: Both the extraction and ground truth contain no spatial relations, so the model correctly matches this aspect.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is mostly correct and informative but is penalized for the incorrect age label and missing track id.

justifications.completeness: Captured the pedestrian and most attributes (action and contexts) but missed the correct age and an object track id, so it is incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic semantic labels (action and context) and an extra track hint but lacks age accuracy and additional contextual details, reducing semantic value.
justifications.spatial_relations: Matches the ground truth by providing no formal spatial_relations and even includes an informal 'on the right' hint, so spatial annotation is effectively correct.
justifications.overall: Mostly correct on action and context with adequate spatial information, but the incorrect age label and missing track identifier lower the overall quality.
